hook: CBC World and News24 World — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Strait of Hormuz / US-Iran deal. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 35 buckets, 62 articles, 8 distinct frames in today's coverage.
Frame: CEASEFIRE_INTEGRITY_DISPUTE (7 buckets). "US attack on Iran tankers clear violation of ceasefire, int’l law: FM spox" — IRNA English
Frame: GLOBAL_OIL_AND_MARKET_SHOCK (6 buckets). "Oil jumps, stocks fall as US-Iran clashes spark peace talks fears" — Iraqi News
Frame: CIA_BLOCKADE_ENDURANCE_ESTIMATE (4 buckets). "CIA analysis suggests Iran could withstand blockade for 4 more months" — CBC World

Frame: TRUMP_THREAT_AS_ESCALATION_DRIVER (3 buckets). "As he struggles to force Iran’s capitulation, US President Donald Trump issued what seemed to be yet another threat to commit an act of mas…" — Asia Times

The paradox: A Western-allied press (Canada) and a non-aligned Global South press (South Africa) both chose to foreground the CIA's own admission that its blockade strategy lacks leverage — a US-intelligence leak that undercuts Washington's position regardless of which side of t…

Silence as data: `iran_state` didn't cover this — they covered Iran's IRNA issued only official legal condemnations of US tanker strikes; no coverage of the substance, terms, or Iranian position on the US peace proposal. instead.

**Asia Times** (asia_pacific_regional): Only outlet to describe Trump's negotiating posture as 'genocidal threats' including promising that Iran's 'whole civilization would die' — language absent from every Western, Gulf, and Iranian outlet.
